threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think waiting for a bus is an option instead of just grabbing an Uber or jumping on a bike. Classic newbie mistake!
Riding the 59 at 2 AM, I saw a guy trying to barter a half-eaten kebab for a ticket. The driver just shook his head, smiling.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: One morning, I saw an old man feeding pigeons while a group of kids practiced breakdancing to a saxophonist's tune. That’s Neukölln in a nutshell.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you sip coffee at a café where the barista also DJs at night. The vibe shifts from chill to euphoric as the sun sets.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know you're a local when you instinctively step over that cracked sidewalk panel on 16th—it’s a rite of passage to avoid the tumble.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 Polk transporting a street performer juggling flaming clubs. Only in SF do rush hour and circus acts collide like that.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think 'Gràcia' is just about Gaudí. But our real gems are the hidden plazas where locals gather, laughing over vermut and homemade tapas.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their bewilderment at the number of people queueing for a table at 8 PM. Only locals know dinner starts late here.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 149 bus is just a convenient ride instead of a rolling social experiment that tests your patience daily.
Caught a late 77 last weekend; a guy was reciting Shakespeare to a silent bus full of drunks. Only in Brixton, right? Perfectly bizarre.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy busking with a saxophone while his dogs playfully tugged at a skateboard in the Görlitzer Park sun. That vibe is what I love.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can hear techno music blaring from a bus stop in Friedrichshain at 3 AM, and nobody bats an eye. It's just another night in Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they know to avoid BART on Fridays; the broken brakes and delays are practically a tradition now.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 31 next to a guy with a surfboard, debating whether to hit Ocean Beach or just head home. Classic SF vibes.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists assume all locals flock to Park Güell, but real Gràcians prefer the quiet charm of Plaça de la Vila. Less photo ops, more everyday life.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still gaping at the street performers, trying to figure out the metro system without Google Maps open.
